metadata.name: NetBird
metadata.description: Self-hosted NetBird control plane with embedded local user management and optional Traefik integration.
metadata.guide:
## Overview
Runs NetBird's current combined self-hosted deployment with embedded/local authentication.

This template intentionally does not expose external IdP configuration or the optional NetBird Proxy service. It focuses on the simple combined deployment: dashboard + `netbird-server`, with optional Traefik integration.

## Traefik Modes
- `traefik_enabled=false`: no Traefik labels are rendered. The stack exposes `127.0.0.1:8080` for the dashboard and `127.0.0.1:8081` for `netbird-server`, which is suitable for another local reverse proxy.
- `traefik_mode=integrated`: starts a bundled Traefik instance inside this stack. When `traefik_tls_enabled=true`, it also handles ACME/TLS directly with the configured `traefik_tls_certresolver`.
- `traefik_mode=existing`: does not start Traefik. Instead, the services join the external `traefik_network` and publish Traefik labels for an already-running Traefik installation, using either HTTP or the configured TLS entrypoint depending on `traefik_tls_enabled`.

## Config Files
- The template renders `compose.yaml` and `config/config.yaml`.
- `config/config.yaml` contains the combined `netbird-server` configuration and the embedded local-auth settings.
- Dashboard settings are rendered directly into `compose.yaml`.
- Keep `config/config.yaml` protected because it contains the relay secret and store encryption key.

## Prerequisites
- A public DNS record for `public_hostname` pointing at the Docker host.
- Publicly reachable UDP port `stun_port`.
- Docker Compose v2 on the target host.
- If `traefik_mode=integrated` and `traefik_tls_enabled=true`, public TCP ports `80` and `443` must reach the host and Let's Encrypt must be able to validate the hostname.
- If `traefik_mode=existing`, the external Docker network named by `traefik_network` must already exist and be shared with your Traefik deployment.
- `netbird_store_encryption_key` must be valid standard base64 that decodes to exactly 32 bytes. The template uses base64 autogeneration for this because NetBird rejects generic random strings for the store encryption key.

## Deploy
- Render `compose.yaml` and `config/config.yaml`.
- Review the hostname, Traefik mode, TLS setting, relay secret, and store encryption key before first start.
- If you use integrated Traefik with TLS enabled, also verify the ACME email and that ports `80` and `443` are free.
- Start the stack with `docker compose up -d`.

## First Login
- On a fresh data volume, open `https://<< public_hostname >>/setup` after the stack is healthy, or use `http://<< public_hostname >>/setup` when `traefik_tls_enabled=false`, and create the first owner account in the NetBird setup wizard.
- On later restarts with the same data volume, NetBird uses the stored users and does not show `/setup` again.

## Notes
- Authentication is handled only by NetBird's embedded IdP (`localAuthDisabled=false`).
- The optional NetBird Proxy service is intentionally omitted from this template.
- `traefik_tls_enabled` now controls whether the rendered public URLs and Traefik labels use HTTPS or HTTP when Traefik is enabled.
- Back up both the NetBird data volume and the rendered `config/config.yaml`, and if you use integrated Traefik with TLS enabled, also back up the Traefik ACME volume.

## References
- [Self-Hosting Quickstart](https://docs.netbird.io/selfhosted/selfhosted-quickstart)
- [Configuration Files Reference](https://docs.netbird.io/selfhosted/configuration-files)
- [Local User Management](https://docs.netbird.io/selfhosted/identity-providers/local)
- [Combined Server Example Config](https://github.com/netbirdio/netbird/blob/main/combined/config.yaml.example)

items.name: public_hostname
items.title: Public Hostname
items.description: Public DNS hostname for the NetBird dashboard and API.

items.name: stun_port
items.title: STUN UDP Port
items.description: Public UDP port exposed for the embedded STUN service.

items.name: netbird_log_level
items.title: Log Level
items.description: Log level used by the combined NetBird server.

items.name: netbird_relay_auth_secret
items.title: Relay Auth Secret
items.description: Shared secret used internally by the embedded relay service.

items.name: netbird_store_encryption_key
items.title: Store Encryption Key
items.description: Base64-encoded 32-byte key used to protect local user data stored by the embedded IdP.

items.name: data_volume_name
items.title: NetBird Data Volume
items.description: Docker volume name for NetBird state, SQLite data, and embedded IdP files.

items.name: traefik_enabled
items.title: Traefik
items.description: Enable Traefik-based ingress instead of localhost-exposed backend ports.

items.name: traefik_mode
items.title: Traefik Mode
items.description: Use `integrated` to start a new bundled Traefik instance, or `existing` to attach NetBird to an already-running Traefik installation.

items.name: traefik_network
items.title: Traefik Network
items.description: External Docker network shared with the existing Traefik installation.

items.name: traefik_tls_entrypoint
items.title: TLS Entrypoint
items.description: Secure Traefik entrypoint name used by the NetBird routers.

items.name: traefik_tls_certresolver
items.title: Cert Resolver
items.description: Traefik certificate resolver name used by the NetBird routers.

items.name: letsencrypt_email
items.title: ACME Email
items.description: Email used for Let's Encrypt registration and expiry notices when the bundled Traefik instance is enabled.

items.name: letsencrypt_volume_name
items.title: Traefik ACME Volume
items.description: Docker volume name for Traefik ACME certificate storage.
